description_long: This object consists of two large, elongated green leaves with prominent parallel veins, attached to thick, upright stems. The leaves are broad, slightly curved, and have a natural, organic waviness along their edges, resembling the foliage of tropical plants. The coloration transitions from a lighter green at the edges to a deeper green toward the center, with subtle gradients and natural imperfections. The stems are cylindrical and robust, supporting the leaves in an upright and slightly splayed arrangement. There is no visible pot or container, and the base of the stems appears to be cut or unfinished. The overall appearance suggests a decorative plant element, likely intended to mimic a banana or bird of paradise plant.
description: Two large, wavy green leaves with thick stems, resembling tropical foliage. No visible pot or container is present.
description_view_0: Two broad green leaves, one angled left and the other upright, with visible stem bases and pronounced leaf veins.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the length and curvature of the left leaf, with the right leaf partially obscured.
description_view_2: Frontal view showing both leaves fully, upright and splayed, with clear vein patterns and thick stems.
description_view_3: Angled view emphasizing the overlapping leaves and the natural waviness of their edges.
attribute_plant_species: banana plant (Musa) or bird of paradise (Strelitzia) foliage
